summary: EXP-225 resolves all three WIN-040 c3 moderate holes in a single two-anchor patch. (A) PREPEND a new WIN-040-specific opening paragraph to detail_evidence that engages the 45°W threshold directly (cell centre ~80°W, westward drift 0.2–0.3°/yr, >100-year falsifiability horizon — ISS-1402) and states the structural axial-symmetry argument that H(r) = 8537·exp(−r/8619) has zero azimuthal derivative, so any longitude-specific SAA feature must be imported from WMM/CHAOS-7 rather than derived from dome geometry (ISS-1403, the c3 curmudgeon's "strongest physics-level argument"). The paragraph preemptively defuses the "you also post-hoc" advocate-mode comeback by reframing the discriminator as "derivation path exists vs. does not" rather than "prediction was pre-registered," citing Terra-Nova et al. 2017 for the standard-MHD derivation path the dome lacks. (B) REPLACE the generic post-hoc boilerplate sentence in detail_verdict_text with a WIN-040-localized version naming the actual confirming sources (WMM-2025 / CHAOS-7 SAA cell-position outputs) and the actual threshold (45°W) — removing the irrelevant "ocean-tide gauges, telluric records" datasets that prompted ISS-1404. Both anchors verified unique. TLDRs unchanged. Verdict "Std Model Explains" unchanged. detail_evidence grows ~931 → ~2,300 chars; detail_verdict_text shrinks ~1,079 → ~1,170 chars (localization is slightly longer than the original boilerplate but cleaner).
additional_recommendations: Consider queuing analogous boilerplate-localization patches for sibling WINs in the 040–043, 059–061, 063 group — the "ocean-tide gauges, telluric records" list likely appears verbatim in their detail_verdict_text boilerplates too. The c3 curmudgeon explicitly flagged this as "a pattern fix, not a one-off."

The axial-symmetry structural argument generalizes beyond WIN-040. Any other WIN that claims a longitude- or azimuth-specific prediction (SAA cell positions, local moon longitude effects, latitude-dependent aetheric refraction that varies with longitude) is subject to the same "H(r) has zero azimuthal derivative" rebuttal. Consider a §6 appendix entry or Section 1.x cross-reference systematizing this.

This patch lowers the WIN-040 advocate-mode defense rating from 3/5 to ~2/5 (the remaining defender moves — "threshold was chosen conservatively" and "dome could add azimuthal structure in a future version" — do not defend the current claim). A follow-up c4 review should confirm the new paragraph fully neutralizes the prior "non-discriminating only" objection.
